selected_tasks.body_preview: - Self-test on Day 110: `yyds state graph clusters` shows the usage line requires `<event-id|patch-id|eval-id|commit>` but does not tell the user how to discover valid IDs. The assessment recorded: "UX friction: help doesn't explain what arguments to pass." - The current error path (line 466 of src/commands_state_graph.rs) prints a wall of usage text without
selected_tasks.expected_evidence: Assessment self-tests will show `state graph clusters` gives helpful guidance Future users won't get stuck on the "what ID do I use" question No new test failures or command regressions Implementation Notes: The dispatch for `graph clusters` is at line 69 of `src/commands_state_graph.rs`: ```rust if args.get(3).map(|arg| arg.as_str()) == Some("clusters") { `
selected_tasks.title: Improve `state graph clusters` discoverability with ID discovery hints

selected_tasks.body_preview: - Trajectory graph pressure (Day 110): `deepseek_cache_ratio_unverified_count=1` — a DeepSeek cache hit ratio was reported in prose without token-backed cache metrics to independently verify it. - `scripts/log_feedback.py` line 2548 self-tests this: `check("cache prose ratio marked unverified", cache_prose["deepseek_cache_ratio_unverified_count"] == 1, cache
selected_tasks.expected_evidence: A new `is_token_backed()` method on `DeepSeekUsage` with unit tests covering all three cases (both Some, one Some, both None) Future log_feedback.py or dashboard code can call this method (via state events that record DeepSeekUsage) to flag unverified ratios The `deepseek_cache_ratio_unverified_count` metric will become more reliable when downstream code con
selected_tasks.title: Add `is_token_backed()` method to DeepSeekUsage for cache ratio verification

selected_tasks.body_preview: - Trajectory graph pressure #1: `transcript_only_failed_tool_count=2` — transcript logs contain tool failures absent from state events - Trajectory graph pressure #2: `state_only_failed_tool_count=13` — state events contain tool failures unmatched in transcript records - These are computed by `scripts/build_evolution_dashboard.py` line 2666-2667 using `uniqu
selected_tasks.expected_evidence: `state failures --recent` output organized by run ID, enabling per-session cross-reference with transcripts The session grouping surfaces which sessions have the most tool failures, highlighting candidates for reconciliation investigation Future dashboard scoring or `log_feedback.py` can consume per-session failure counts from this command Implementation Not
selected_tasks.title: Add per-session tool failure grouping to `state failures --recent`
